name: Game Bird Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 lb (or up to 7 pounds) game birds, cut into pieces
1 quart water (or stock), cooled slightly from boiling
3 wild celery stalks, cut into long pieces
1 1/2 cups fresh garden peas (or chopped day lily buds)
1 tsp coltsfoot ash {substitute salt in same amount}
recipeInstructions:
This recipe is suitable for most game birds. Ensure the stew doesn't boil while the meat is in the liquid.
1. Place game birds in a stew pot with the water. Add celery and wild onions. Simmer slowly for 1 hour, removing any scum from the surface.
2. At the end of the first hour, add peas, peppergrass seeds, cayenne, coltsfoot ash, and lemon juice.
3. Continue simmering for another hour. Do not allow to boil.
4. Remove meat and vegetables from stock and keep warm. Bring the stock to a rapid boil. Boil for 20 minutes. Strain.
5. Place flour in a small screw-top jar. Add cold water, cover, and shake to blend thoroughly. Slowly pour this mixture into the stock while stirring. Cook over moderate heat until thickened as desired.
6. Return solid food to the thickened stock and serve over steamed rice.
Yield: 6 servings.
